meaning: adv.令人恐惧地,引起突然惊恐地
examples: But life in modern America is punctuated frighteningly often by such attacks.
Energy, though not as frighteningly expensive as in 2008, is also no longer cheap.
Although SNMP is frighteningly complex to set up and use, the stack discussed in this article makes it fun.
Frighteningly, both are armed with the latest technologies and the winner is far from predictable.
But the network vulnerability that Anton Kapela and Alex Pilosov will present is frighteningly simple.
